summary: Compilation warnings about deprecated/removed `FailureExpected` API in multiple testing framework classes, combined with test assertion failures (assertFalse) in the stack trace, causing the build to exit with code 1.
root_cause: The `FailureExpected` class or methods are marked with `[removal]` deprecation, indicating they have been deprecated and marked for removal. Multiple files in the test framework (ExtendedFrameworkMethod.java, BaseNonConfigCoreFunctionalTestCase.java, Helper.java, CustomRunner.java) continue to use this removed/deprecated API, generating compiler warnings and likely causing test assertion failures.

evidence.reason: Multiple instances show FailureExpected class marked as removed/deprecated, appearing across multiple test framework files
Code continues to declare and use the deprecated FailureExpected type despite removal warnings
Stack trace shows test assertion failures occurring during execution
Build failed with exit code 1, confirming failure

suggested_fix: Update the testing framework classes to replace all uses of the deprecated/removed `FailureExpected` API with its replacement or modern equivalent. Review the Java version upgrade notes or framework documentation to identify the correct replacement for `FailureExpected` functionality.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
